summary: Executed Phases 1-7 of the NixOS doc/config improvement plan: fixed the nandi/hamsa CPU-vendor factual bug in README.md, de-staled docs/configuration.md, docs/unstable-packages.md, docs/how-to-add-package.md, and docs/how-to-add-service.md to describe the current post-reorg modules/system + modules/home layout, corrected packages/README.md and docs/packages.md (removed nonexistent marker-pdf.nix, added opencode/kooha/slidev sections, python312 -> python3), swept emoji glyphs from eligible docs/*.md files, made three conservative comment-only config cleanups in modules/system/packages.nix and home.nix (verified via nix flake check), and created overlays/README.md closing the last directory-convention gap. Phase 8 (Ryzen doc consolidation, niri.md testing-phase framing, niri.md emoji strip) was deliberately left untouched per the orchestration directive, since all three items require explicit user judgment.
continuation_context: Phase 8 remains [NOT STARTED] by design (deferred, not executed under autonomous orchestration). Recommend spawning a follow-up task to handle: (1) Ryzen doc consolidation (docs/ryzen-ai-300-compatibility.md + docs/ryzen-ai-300-support-summary.md near-duplicates), (2) docs/niri.md 'Recommended Usage Strategy' testing-phase framing re-confirmation against actual daily-driver usage, (3) a dedicated docs/niri.md emoji-strip task (~58 glyphs, 1035 lines).
